page: OVHcloud d2-4 is a virtual server instance belonging to the Discovery family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz on an Intel x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is defined by a 0 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations place the performance of this instance in the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, fall within the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is also limited, measuring 133.34 GB/sec for cached small blocks and dropping to 19.19 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as the Passmark CPU encryption test and single-threaded gzip compression, similarly rank in the bottom 10%.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and lower-tier benchmark results, this instance presents clear performance tradeoffs. It is not configured with a GPU, and its processing capabilities are constrained, as shown by a weak rating in small-model LLM text generation and poor ratings in database operations and Redis SET operations. Consequently, the d2-4 is structured for low-demand workloads such as basic development environments, light testing, or simple static web serving, where resource density and qualitative cost efficiency are prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: OVHcloud d2-4 is an entry-level virtual server from the Discovery family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs on an Intel x86_64 architecture with 4.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance includes one public IPv4 address but operates with a 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Across multiple benchmarks, including stress-ng, Geekbench, and memory bandwidth tests, the server consistently performs in the bottom 10% tier. This shared-CPU configuration offers a budget-friendly resource profile with clear performance tradeoffs, making it suitable primarily for lightweight tasks. It is best utilized for basic software development, small-scale testing, or low-traffic static web serving rather than resource-intensive database operations or complex computational workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud d2-4 is a Discovery family virtual server featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for entry-level workloads and basic testing.
meta_description: OVHcloud d2-4 is a budget-focused virtual server with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe storage for basic development tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server with shared CPU resources and local NVMe storage, designed for lightweight development and basic testing environments.
bullet_points: Features 2 shared Intel vCPUs at 2.0 GHz and 4.0 GB of RAM
Includes 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Equipped with one complimentary public IPv4 address
Performance benchmarks rank in the bottom 10% tier
Suited for basic development, light testing, and static web serving
